job_text: COMPANY BACKGROUNDHaier is the number one brand of Major Appliances in the world with 16.3 percent of global market share. Headquartered in Qingdao, Haier has over 80,000 employees across 30 countries in the world.Haier Singapore is one of the holding companies under Haier Group. As the integrated regional centre, Haier Singapore is the main platform to provide Trading, R&D, Big Data, Cybersecurity, Internal Audit and Legal supports to over 80 countries in Asia, Europe, Africa and North America markets.As the continuous demands from global businesses, we are looking for passionate Data Engineer to join our global big data team.KEY RESPONSIBILITIESBased in Singapore, you will be part of the global big data team and responsible for the development, maintenance, integration, data governance and data reporting of Haier’s overseas business systems, including:Data Integration and ETL:Design and develop ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes using Azure Data Factory, Databricks & SSIS.Integrate various data sources such as databases, APIs, and file systems into a unified data platform.Ensure data quality, consistency, and accuracy during the data integration process.Data Storage and Management:Set up and manage data storage solutions using Azure Data Lake Storage, Azure Blob Storage, and Azure SQL Database.Implement data partitioning, indexing, and optimization strategies for efficient data retrieval and storage.Monitor and maintain data storage infrastructure to ensure reliability and performance.Data Processing and Transformation:Develop and maintain data processing pipelines using Databricks and Spark.Implement data transformation logic to convert raw data into meaningful insights.Optimize data processing workflows for performance and scalabilityData Analysis and Reporting:Collaborate with data scientists and analysts to develop data models and analytical solutions.Create and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Power BI.Perform ad-hoc data analysis to support business decision-making. AI/Machine Learning Model DevelopmentDevelop, train, and optimize machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI modelsSelect appropriate algorithms, features, and architectures based on business problemsPerform model evaluation, tuning, and validation to ensure accuracy, robustness, and fairnessSecurity and Compliance:Implement security best practices to protect data at rest and in transit.Ensure compliance with data privacy regulations and organizational policies.Monitor and audit data access and usage to detect and respond to security incidents.Collaboration and Communication:Work closely with cross-functional teams including data scientists, software engineers, and business stakeholders.Communicate technical concepts and project status to non-technical stakeholders.Performance Monitoring and Optimization:Monitor the performance of data pipelines, storage solutions, and analytical workloads.Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks to ensure efficient data processing.Implement automation and orchestration tools to streamline data workflows.Continuous Improvement:Stay updated with the latest trends and advancements in Azure and Databricks technologies.Evaluate and recommend new tools and technologies to improve data engineering processes.Participate in training and development activities to enhance technical skills.Documentation and Knowledge Sharing:Maintain comprehensive documentation of data pipelines, processes, and architectures.Share knowledge and best practices with the team through presentations, workshops, and documentation.Ensure that all data engineering activities are well-documented and easily accessible.These responsibilities ensure that the data infrastructure is robust, scalable, and capable of supporting the organization’s data needs.KEY REQUIREMENTSThe ideal candidates we are looking for should be seasoned Business Intelligence & AL/ML Professionals with:Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field.With extensive experience in business intelligence, AI/ML (LLM) and object-oriented development within a commercial environment.Familiar with Hadoop (Cloudera distribution), Hive / Impala, Spark, Python, & R.Experience in cloud platforms (Microsoft Azure and AWS cloud services), RESTful APIs and microservicesExperience in databases (Azure, Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL) and enterprise ERP/CRM (SAP, MS Dynamics 365, Salesforce)Able to demonstrate solid business understanding in complex B2B and B2C sales environment.Good people skills and able to work with people at all levels.Strong communication, presentation and interpersonal skills.

job_text: SSG is a dynamic and forward-thinking organization dedicated to empowering individuals and shaping the future of Singapore's workforce. As the National Skills Authority, SSG leads the charge in driving the SkillsFuture movement, a national initiative that promotes lifelong learning and skills development. With a strong focus on innovation and collaboration, SSG works closely with employers, training providers, and individuals to create a vibrant ecosystem of learning and growth. By offering a wide range of initiatives, programs, and funding schemes, SSG enables individuals to unlock their full potential, acquire new competencies, and stay ahead in a rapidly changing job market. The National Jobs-Skills Data Office, under the Skills Intelligence and Planning Division, serves three core functions:·      Data and Algorithms Innovation and R&D: Undertake development of new data models and algorithms to serve whole of government’s jobs-skills intelligence needs; ·      Jobs-Skills Product Management, Development and Delivery: Manage and enhance jobs-skills products, which includes UX/UI design and end-to-end product life cycle  management;·      Data Management and Operations: Centrally manage data quality, data models and  data infrastructure to support internal and external usersCome join this game-changing team in SSG, where we empower employers, citizens, training providers, and policymakers to make informed decisions by using a user-centered approach, providing trusted source of jobs-skills data and insights, and a common set of jobs-skills taxonomies for a skills-first future!As a Data Scientist, you will be a key member of the Data and Algorithms Innovation and R&D team, transforming complex data into meaningful insights that drive strategic decisions, building statistical, machine learning and graph models to solve real-world problems, designing experiments to measure impact and communicating findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. By combining analytical thinking, programming skills and policy understanding, you will help uncover opportunities, solve real-world problems and guide data-informed decision-making across SSG.·       Develop and deploy advanced analytical models to extract deeper insights from jobs, skills, and tasks data, applying statistical and machine learning techniques including feature engineering, model selection, and validation using appropriate performance metrics·       Design and build scalable data infrastructure by creating modular, reusable data pipelines and prototyping advanced analytics solutions that operationalize insights to support evolving stakeholder needs across various partnership levels·       Translate complex data signals into strategic insights through close collaboration with product and policy teams, ensuring analytical outputs meaningfully inform workforce and industry planning decisions that align with national skills development objectives·       Drive continuous analytical improvement by evaluating and enhancing existingmethodologies, incorporating new data sources, external datasets, and robust model validation frameworks to strengthen the analytical foundation·       Lead analytical partnerships and collaborations by scoping and supporting projects with internal divisions and external partners, translating stakeholder requirements into well-defined data science problem statements and delivering comprehensive analytical solutions·       Contribute to national jobs-skills data assets including the development of structured taxonomies, entity extraction pipelines, and knowledge graph representations that support Singapore's skills intelligence ecosystem·       Champion research-backed innovation by staying current with emerging analytical techniques, tools, and methodologies, ensuring SIPD maintains cutting-edge capabilities in workforce analytics and skills intelligenceRequirementsExperience:Background in data science, statistics, machine learning, or related quantitative disciplines with demonstrated experience applying analytical techniques to both structured and unstructured datasetsProficiency in Python and SQL for data analysis, modeling, and data pipeline development, with experience in machine learning model deployment or productionization Experience with unstructured data processing, particularly text or language data using natural language processing, language models, or text analytics techniquesProven track record in developing, evaluating, and validating statistical or machine learning models using appropriate validation approaches and performance metricsFamiliarity with data visualization tools and techniques for presenting complex analytical insights to diverse audiencesKnowledge of labour market, workforce, or skills-related data and analyticsAnalytical and Problem-Solving Competencies:Strong analytical mindset with ability to break down complex or ambiguous problems into structured analyses and testable hypothesesExperience in translating business requirements into technical solutions and communicating findings effectively to diverse stakeholder groupsCollaboration and Communication Skills:Excellent communication abilities to explain analytical findings and technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders, including policy makers and senior leadershipSelf-driven contributor with strong ownership of analytical workstreams and proven ability to collaborate effectively across product, research, and policy teamsExperience in managing multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining high standards of analytical rigor and delivery qualitySuccessful candidates will be offered a 2-year contract in the first instance and may be considered for an extension or be placed on a permanent tenure.Candidates are encouraged to sign up for a Careers & Skills Passport (CSP) account and include your CSP public profile in your resume. Please check out www.myskillsfuture.gov.sg for details on the CSP. NCCS Data and Computational Science (DCS) is a newly established computational hub within National Cancer Center of Singapore (NCCS) which focuses on leveraging data analytics and computational methods to advance cancer research and treatment. DCS features high-powered computing resources capable of processing ‘big data’ profiles and running advanced interpretable machine learning algorithms and robust statistical techniques. DCS offers in-house and centralised solutions for NCCS researchers who require computational analysis without the need to buy specialised equipment or contract with third party vendors. DCS aims to maximise the efficiency of data processes and accelerate research outcomes. With access to national level medical data spanning clinical, imaging and omics datasets, our efforts are concentrated on harvesting the innate value of these rich datasets to improve cancer patient care and treatment delivery through the production of world-class research.The Data Scientist will provide support and insights for DCS core multi-omics research and big data analysis computing infrastructure that focus on using next-generation sequencing (NGS), radiological imaging and other multi-modal datatypes to develop biomarkers predictive of clinical responses in cancer patients. The Data Scientist will be expected to wrangle and manage large datasets, execute computational pipelines and interpret data to better understand the complexity of cancer progression and treatment resistance across multiple cancer types, as well as design new pipelines and/or optimise existing ones to meet evolving research needs.Key Responsibilities:- Drive and manage multiple projects: plan and perform sequencing data analyses, statistical analyses, and other relevant computational analyses and interpret the results in the context of assigned research projects/questions.- Collaboration: Work closely with the Principal Investigator, clinicians, data scientists, researchers and stakeholders.- Data pipeline management: Design, implement and maintain data processing pipelines for ingesting, transforming, and loading data from various sources.- Security and Compliance: Define computing resources and data access controls, encryption, and authentication mechanisms. Ensure compliance with data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR, PDPA, APAC Data Laws etc.) and organisation structures.- IT Infrastructure Maintenance: Monitor system performance, identify and resolve bottlenecks or issues, ensuring minimal downtime. Apply software updates and patches. Backup data to prevent data loss. Source and liaise with vendors in procurement of the IT infrastructure to support the team's expansion needs as necessary.Job Requirements:- Master/PhD in Computational Biology or Computer Science or Mathematics or Biostatistics or Physics preferred.- At least 3 years research experience in a Genomics-related field is mandatory.- Knowledge and experience in omics analyses is mandatory (any of WGS, WES, RNAseq, single-cell sequencing or similar).- Knowledge and experience in biostatistical analyses of clinical datasets preferred.- Familiarity with data security and access control measures preferred.- Ability to plan and execute data analysis and ad-hoc projects, both independently and in collaboration with external parties.- Strong organisational, interpersonal and presentation skills.- Familiarity with Linux or other Unix flavours, preferably as an administrator/superuser and server maintenance preferred.- Familiarity with pipeline management systems (e.g. Nextflow), job schedulers (e.g. Slurm), container/virtualization systems (Docker, Singularity). We are passionate about this and hope you are too.About the Team:TikTok LIVE Data Platform is building next-generation Data & AI infrastructure, including the Index Platform, Label Platform, Portrait Platform, Diagnostic Platform, Data Asset Management Platform, Data Suite, Data Agent and AI-native solutions for internal and business users.Our platform powers data products across TikTok LIVE, covering data processing (streaming & batch), OLAP analytics, data visualization, AI-powered analytics, intelligent diagnostics, and AI Agent workflows. products that empower millions of users and internal stakeholders.ResponsibilitiesDesign, develop, and maintain full-stack applications for TikTok LIVE Data Platform, including web applications, business efficiency and decision making.Collaborate with product, data, AI, and infrastructure teams to deliver scalable, reliable, and high-performance solutions across frontend, backend, and cloud engineering excellence through reusable architecture, developer tooling, testing, performance optimization, and best practices.QualificationsMinimum QualificationsBachelor’s degree or above in Computer Science or a related technical discipline.Solid software engineering fundamentals with experience developing modern web applications using at least one mainstream frontend framework (React, Vue, Angular, etc.).Experience building backend services and APIs with at least one backend language (Java, Go, Python, Node.js, etc.), with understanding of distributed systems and service-oriented architecture.Familiar with frontend engineering, component-based development, API design, and modern software development practices.Good understanding of databases, data modeling, and common storage technologies (SQL/NoSQL).Strong problem-solving ability, communication skills, and willingness to learn new technologies in a fast-paced environment.Good written and spoken English for cross-functional and cross-regional collaboration.Preferred QualificationsCandidates with one or more of the following experiences are highly preferred:Experience building data platforms, data infrastructure, or data-intensive applications, with familiarity in technologies such as Spark, Flink, Kafka, ClickHouse, Doris, Elasticsearch, or similar systems.Experience developing data visualization platforms, BI systems, dashboards, or analytical applications using modern visualization frameworks.Hands-on experience building AI-native applications or AI Agents, with practical knowledge of Agent architectures, orchestration frameworks, tool calling, memory, planning, and evaluation methodologies.

job_text: SSG is a dynamic and forward-thinking organization dedicated to empowering individuals and shaping the future of Singapore's workforce. As the National Skills Authority, SSG leads the charge in driving the SkillsFuture movement, a national initiative that promotes lifelong learning and skills development. With a strong focus on innovation and collaboration, SSG works closely with employers, training providers, and individuals to create a vibrant ecosystem of learning and growth. By offering a wide range of initiatives, programs, and funding schemes, SSG enables individuals to unlock their full potential, acquire new competencies, and stay ahead in a rapidly changing job market. The National Jobs-Skills Data Office, under the Skills Intelligence and Planning Division, serves three core functions:·      Data and Algorithms Innovation and R&D: Undertake development of new data models and algorithms to serve whole of government’s jobs-skills intelligence needs; ·      Jobs-Skills Product Management, Development and Delivery: Manage and enhance jobs-skills products, which includes UX/UI design and end-to-end product life cycle management;·      Data Management and Operations: Centrally manage data quality, data models and data infrastructure to support internal and external usersCome join this game-changing team in SSG, where we empower employers, citizens, training providers, and policymakers tomake informed decisions by using a user-centered approach, providing trusted source of jobs-skills data and insights, and a common set of jobs-skills taxonomies for a skills-first future!As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will be a key member of the Data Management and Operations team, designing, building and deploying scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. You will be creating robust data pipelines and integrating models into production environments. Working closely with data scientists, data engineers and software engineers, you will ensure that machine learning solutions are reliable, efficient and continuously monitored to maintain performance over time.• Design and implement scalable AI/ML infrastructure by aligning data warehouses, APIs, and downstream systems under a governed, scalable model that ensures seamless integration with existing systems while maintaining high performance and reliability standards• Develop and deploy robust AI solutions including the design, development, and deployment of AI models that integrate seamlessly with existing systems, while evaluating and integrating third-party AI tools and frameworks to enhance analytical capabilities• Optimize and maintain ML model performance through continuous fine-tuning of existing AI models for performance, accuracy, and scalability, implementing automated monitoring systems for model performance including drift detection, latency monitoring, and resource utilization tracking• Build and maintain end-to-end data architecture by designing robust data systems that integrate ingestion, metadata, storage, and consumption layers across production job_text: OverviewThe Consortium for Clinical Research and Innovation, Singapore (CRIS) brings together seven national R&D, clinical translation and service programmes to advance clinical research and innovation for Singapore and establish important capabilities for a future-ready healthcare system.The Business Entities under CRIS include:Singapore Clinical Research Institute (SCRI)National Health Innovation Centre (NHIC)Advanced Cell Therapy and Research Institute, Singapore (ACTRIS)Precision Health Research, Singapore (PRECISE)Singapore Translational Cancer Consortium (STCC)Cardiovascular Disease National Collaborative Enterprise (CADENCE)SIngapore Medical FOundation AI model (SIMFONI)PRECISE is the national central entity coordinating Singapore’s multi-phase National Precision Medicine (NPM) programme. familiarity with statistical genetics is an advantage.Working knowledge of scripting or data analysis tools (e.g., Python, R, Bash, Nextflow, Jupyter Notebooks) for hands-on pipeline support and process automation.Experience working in TRE or other secure data environments is preferred.Familiarity with cloud-based platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP) is an advantage.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail.Good verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to work with both technical and non-technical stakeholders and to prepare clear user-facing guidance and documentation.What you need to knowSuccessful candidate will be offered a 3-year renewable contract. job_text: Team IntroductionThe AI Data and Safety team plays a critical role in advancing Seed foundational models across modalities and improving AI-native applications built on the Seed model series. We work across the data lifecycle, from defining evaluation approaches and translating user feedback and benchmark outcomes into data requests, to building scalable processes that improve data quality and support rapid model iteration.Our team combines technical and operational capabilities, bringing together multidisciplinary talent across product management, data engineering, and data operations. Our work is driven by people who think deeply about model behavior, move quickly to solve complex problems, and bring first-hand experience as both builders and users of models and agents.In close partnership with Seed researchers, industry experts, and leading data vendors, we tackle challenging data problems at the frontier of AI development, helping improve both model performance and user experience.AI Data & Safety - External Partnership Development Team is dedicated to continuously sourcing high-quality data suppliers for foundation models and AI-native applications. Based on the data requirements of model training, the team conducts market research and manages the end-to-end process—from supplier sourcing and evaluation, commercial negotiations, and project initiation, to final delivery. By efficiently fulfilling data requests and building a robust supplier ecosystem, the team provides a reliable, high-quality data foundation for the continuous iteration of our models.AI Training Data Market Research and Resource Development1. Partner closely with AI model training teams to understand business needs, define data types, standards, and core requirements, and conduct structured industry and market research to stay abreast of AI data trends, market landscape, and technological advancements2. Translate market insights into high-quality data resource development strategies by identifying and engaging startups and leading data suppliers; establish effective supplier evaluation and screening mechanisms; and expand and onboard suppliers to build a robust, high-quality data ecosystem3. Conduct strategic analysis of market, supplier, and industry data to extract actionable insights; produce analytical reports to support decision-making in AI model training, data procurement, and business strategy4. Continuously identify and monitor emerging data resources and potential partners, maintain strong market relationships, and ensure access to forward-looking, high-quality data sourcesSupplier Onboarding, Delivery, and Lifecycle Management1. Lead supplier onboarding, including evaluation, qualification review, and capability validation, ensuring alignment with project delivery standards and seamless integration into internal workflows2. Manage end-to-end commercial engagements with suppliers, including contract negotiation, terms drafting, cost control, and compliance review, ensuring efficient and compliant partnerships while optimising commercial terms3. Oversee supplier delivery across projects by coordinating internal and external stakeholders, clarifying requirements (even in ambiguous scenarios), managing expectations, and ensuring timely, high-quality data delivery aligned with project goals4. Maintain and develop long-term supplier relationships; regularly evaluate supplier performance and service quality; optimise supplier management frameworks; and leverage data analysis to generate insights and improve supplier performance and collaboration valueQualificationsMinimum Qualifications1. Bachelor’s degree or above in Marketing, Business Management, Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, or related fields; candidates with relevant AI data industry internship or project experience will also be considered.2. At least 2 years of relevant experience in AI data services, data sourcing, supplier/vendor management, or business development; familiarity with AI training data markets, data types, and supplier ecosystems is preferred; experience with leading AI data companies is a plus3. Strong strategic thinking and analytical skills, with the ability to independently conduct market research, synthesise insights, and solve complex problems4. Proven ability in supplier sourcing and channel development, with strong business acumen and the ability to identify and match high-quality resources to business needs5. Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to navigate complex and ambiguous situations across multiple stakeholders6. Basic project management capabilities, with the ability to plan, execute, and monitor supplier onboarding and delivery processes, ensuring timelines, quality, and risk control; strong data analysis and reporting skills, with the ability to derive insights and drive process improvements7. Highly agile, detail-oriented, and execution-focused, with strong ownership, resilience under a challenging environment, and a collaborative mindset

job_text: Project OverviewThis project aims to develop and clinically validate a real-time blood analysis and diagnostic platform using an integrated microfluidic medical device. The platform uses precision microfluidic technology to process and profile the biological activity of white blood cells, which is related to inflammatory conditions ranging from diabetes and sepsis to leukemia.The Postdoctoral Research Associate - Data Scientist will lead the acquisition, preprocessing, analysis, and modelling of medical device-generated data, and will work closely with clinicians, scientists, and engineers to translate AI/machine learning models into working, deployable software for clinical testing and point-of-care use.This role offers a rare opportunity to bridge clinical data science, medical device engineering, and translational research, moving from experimental and clinical sample data to robust AI-enabled diagnostic workflows and deployable prototypes.ResponsibilitiesThe Postdoctoral Research Associate - Data Scientist is responsible for developing end-to-end data and AI/ML workflows for the medical diagnostic platform, from device data acquisition and signal/data preprocessing to model optimisation, validation, software integration, and deployment.Responsibilities include:- Work closely with clinicians, scientists, software engineers, mechanical/electrical engineers, and data scientists to define clinical use cases, data requirements, model outputs, and deployment workflows for the medical device platform.- Design and implement reliable data acquisition pipelines from medical device hardware, sensors, imaging or signal outputs, and associated experimental or clinical metadata.- Develop preprocessing, quality-control, annotation, feature-engineering, and data-management workflows for noisy, multimodal, longitudinal, or time-series biomedical data.- Analyse experimental and clinical sample data to characterise device and biological performance, identify artefacts, benchmark platform performance, and support evidence generation for translational studies.- Develop, train, validate, and optimise machine learning, deep learning, statistical, or hybrid models for diagnostic, predictive, or decision-support tasks using appropriate metrics, cross-validation, robustness checks, and uncertainty analyses.- Translate research models into reproducible and deployable software components, including model inference pipelines, APIs, dashboards, reports, or lightweight user interfaces for clinical and laboratory testing.- Support software-hardware integration for data acquisition, model inference, device feedback, and prototype deployment; assist with basic UX refinement based on clinician and end-user feedback.- Maintain high-quality documentation of datasets, code repositories, model versions, experimental protocols, validation results, data dictionaries, and technical reports.- Prepare manuscripts, presentations, grant reports, and invention disclosures; contribute to literature reviews, patent/IP searches, and responsible research dissemination where relevant.- Support clinical study workflows by coordinating with clinical collaborators and applying good data governance practices for sensitive clinical and experimental data.Requirements- Ph.D. in Data Science, Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Biomedical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Bioinformatics, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Computational Biology, or a related field. Candidates close to completion may be considered if the degree will be conferred before appointment.- Demonstrated research experience in data science, machine learning, deep learning, signal/image processing, biomedical data analysis, time-series analysis, clinical AI, or related computational research.- Strong programming skills in Python; experience with common scientific and ML frameworks such as NumPy, pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch or TensorFlow, Jupyter, and Git/GitHub.- Experience with data acquisition, data cleaning, feature engineering, model optimisation, evaluation, reproducibility, version control, and scientific software development.- Familiarity with clinical or biomedical datasets, medical device data, biosensor/microfluidic data, imaging data, physiological signals, or multimodal data integration is highly advantageous.- Experience translating ML models into usable software prototypes, such as REST APIs, dashboards.- Working knowledge of software-hardware interfacing, instrument-control software, serial/USB/Bluetooth communication, microcontrollers, embedded systems, or edge AI deployment would be advantageous.- Ability to work closely with clinicians and multidisciplinary teams to interpret findings, gather end-user feedback, define clinical workflow needs, and improve the usability of AI/ML outputs.- Strong written and oral communication skills, including the ability to write scientific manuscripts, technical documentation, reports, research proposals, and presentations for technical and non-technical audiences.- Strong commitment to robust, ethical, and responsible AI/ML development, including attention to data privacy, model bias, interpretability, clinical validity, reproducibility, and safe deployment.- Able and committed to work in Singapore, and willing to meet regularly with collaborators at NUS, NUH, and partnering hospitals.Relevant SkillsPython, R/MATLAB, SQL, NumPy, pandas, scikit-learn, PyTorch/TensorFlow, Jupyter, data acquisition, biomedical signal processing, time-series analysis, image processing/computer vision, data preprocessing, feature engineering, model validation, model optimisation, uncertainty/robustness analysis, explainable AI, data visualisation, dashboards, REST APIs, Docker, Linux, Git/GitHub, MLOps, UX prototyping, software-hardware interfacing, and clinical data governance.To apply, please visit our website at: https://portal.smart.mit.edu/careers/career-opportunitiesInterested applicants are invited to send in their full CV/resume, cover letter and list of three references (to include reference names and contact information). We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

COMPANY DESCRIPTION

1-Net manages carrier-neutral Internet Data Centers, along with providing a comprehensive range of integrated services including network connectivity, managed services, work area recovery suite, cloud and media delivery services. 1-Net's Data Center Corridor initiative currently interconnects key data centers in Singapore to facilities quick deployment and providing a diverse connectivity options for enterprises and service providers.
1-Net is fast transforming itself into Asia's Data Center of Choice. As one of the pioneers in broadband development in Singapore, 1-Net currently manages telco-class carrier-neutral Internet Data Centers, along with providing domestic and international connectivity, managed services and media delivery services. 1-Net offers superior stability, secure delivery and service experience through its services. It's uniquely positioned in Southeast Asia, with a comprehensive range of integrated services and a media-centric approach to bring cutting edge solutions to its customers.
We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace where talent thrives. Our hiring decisions are made based on merit and fit-to-role. If you have a disability or special need which requires accommodation to participate in the recruitment process, please inform us when you submit your online application. We will be happy to support as necessary.
Thank you for your interest and application to this role. Please note that only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Facilities Operation and Maintenance:

Conduct the required daily maintenance and operations of the data center facilities to ensure serviceability of all active and redundancy equipment. This includes performing routine facilities activities such as critical system checks and functional testing
Respond to Mechanical and Electrical alarms/faults, identify trends, and implement corrective actions as needed
Respond and investigate Building Management System (BMS)/ Environmental Monitoring System (EMS) alerts, providing feedback on outstanding issues or events, responding to emergency situations, and being on standby duty as required
Support Data Center projects and any additional and alteration work such as site construction activities and installations, coordination with external contractors and testing in adherence to operational standards within the given timeline
Other duties as assigned that are consistent with the responsibilities and objectives of the role

Technical and Customer Support:

Ensure contracted service level agreements and key performance indicators are met or exceeded
Attend to customer requests and deliveries such as installations, coordination with the design & construction, operations requirements to coordinate customer move-in and turn-on, and others

Events and Accreditation Support:

Involve in the scheduling, coordination and completion of all significant planned and emergency maintenance events for the facility and ensure these activities are executed in a controlled and proven method
Undertake assigned tasks to support the various accreditation, certification and compliance initiatives such as BCA-IDA Green Mark DC/QMS/ISMS/BCMS/PCIDSS/OSPAR, etc as may be required
Comply to all standard operating procedures (SOP), method of procedures (MOP), emergency operating procedures (EOP) and emergency response procedures (ERP) established for the critical environments

QUALIFICATIONS

We welcome the following applicants:

Degree graduates in Electrical or Mechanical engineering or equivalent qualifications
Candidates without the required academic credentials but with at least 2 years of relevant working experience in Data Center facilities management and operations or facility management experiences (M&E) in building, construction, semiconductors or equivalent
Candidates with more years of relevant working experience in Data Center facilities management and operations may be considered for the Senior Engineer role

Good technical and functional skills and knowledge of facilities management and operations
Able to commit to 24x7 rotating shift duty
Strong communication skills
Working location: 1-Net (East) at Chai Chee / 1-Net (North) at Woodlands

job_text: About Tara Climate Foundation Tara Climate Foundation is an Asia-based philanthropicorganisation committed to building a more sustainable and prosperous future forthe region. At the heart of our mission lies Asia’s just energy transition –recognising that with half the world’s population and the fastest-growingenergy demand, the region’s progress will shape the world’s collective future. Established in 2021 and headquartered in Singapore, TaraClimate Foundation works with over 355 partners across 12 Asian geographies,including think tanks, research institutes, advisory groups, and associations.Tara provides grants, convenes stakeholders, and facilitates knowledge-sharingto accelerate clean energy development, drive a just transition, and enableclean industrialisation. Underpinning these efforts is a strong commitment tounlocking sustainable finance, supporting climate goals, and aligning businessaction with a people-centred energy transition. Our team of over 50 experts isspread across the Asia-Pacific region, working to accelerate climate action inthis decisive decade. For more on Tara Climate Foundation, visithttps://taraclimate.org/.About the RoleThe world is shifting from fossil fuels to renewable energy. To help shape Tara Climate Foundation’s strategy and support our partners across Asia, we need to gather, interpret and use a wide range of data on the clean energy transition. This is the role of the Senior Data & Impact Officer.We are looking for a highly capable analyst with strong knowledge of the energy transition, excellent data interpretation and communication skills, and a deep commitment to climate action. This role requires someone who can work with detailed datasets while also understanding what the numbers mean for strategy, policy, infrastructure, and real-world decarbonisation pathways.The successful candidate will be able to understand and interpret data across areas such as electricity generation, power sector planning, renewable and fossil fuel energy infrastructure, greenhouse gas emissions, energy efficiency, industrial decarbonisation, finance flows and policy change. They will not only capture and manage data, but also ask the right questions of it: what is changing, what is not changing, where progress is evidenced, where there are gaps, and what this means for Tara’s programs and partners.You will excel at gathering, analysing and interpreting data, and at turning complex evidence into insights for senior leadership, program teams, and funders. You will be comfortable moving between quantitative analysis, interpretation and communication. You will also bring the curiosity and motivation of someone who follows energy transition developments closely and enjoys making sense of technical and policy-oriented information.You will also apply your analytical skills to Tara’s internal organisational data, supporting monitoring, evaluation and learning (MEL) that help us assess progress towards our long-term strategic goals and annual objectives across geographies and sectors.You will work closely with program teams across Asia, support Tara’s programs and data-focused partners and grantees, and contribute to the development of shared data resources, insights and narratives that strengthen climate and energy strategies across the region.Key responsibilitiesClean energy transition data analysis and insights — 40%Working with the Deputy Director for Program Management & Impact, contribute strongly to the development and delivery of Tara’s Program Data Strategy, ensuring it supports Tara’s strategic goals, four-year plan, and program priorities.Support senior management and regional, country and sector program teams to identify priority data, analysis and insight needs.Lead the systematic collection, cleaning, integration and analysis of national, regional and global energy and climate datasets relevant to Tara’s strategy and grant-making.Analyze data on the clean energy transition, including areas such as power sector transformation, renewable energy deployment, fossil fuel phase-down, electricity demand, grid development, industrial decarbonization, energy efficiency, emissions trajectories, finance flows and policy implementation.Interpret quantitative evidence in context, identifying trends, uncertainties, data gaps, strategic implications and opportunities for Tara and its partners.Produce high-quality reports, briefs, dashboards, data visualizations and presentations on a regular cadence.Support Tara teams to use data and evidence in strategy development, program design, partner engagement, funder communications and external storytelling.Translate complex analytical findings into clear, accessible narratives about the progress, challenges and politics of the clean energy transition in Asia.Results-based management and Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning — 40%Support the ongoing implementation of Tara’s Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (MEL) systems, ensuring they support organisational learning, accountability and strategic decision-making.Support Tara’s annual planning processes, including the review of goals and objectives for alignment with the MEL system and approach.Contribute to mid-year and year-end reviews and reporting, drawing out patterns and implications from the level of achievement of goals and objectives across the organisation for future strategy.Contribute strongly to Tara’s annual MEL report based on input from program leads, internal data and relevant external evidence.Support the development of analytics based on Tara’s grant management system, financial management information and other internal systems, where required.Help improve how Tara uses organisational data to understand program effectiveness, risks, and resource allocation.Data-focused grant-making and partner collaboration — 20%Help strengthen Tara’s network of data, research and analytical partners across Asia.Contribute towards grant-making that can improve the availability, quality and use of open-source energy transition data and analysis across the climate ecosystem. Propose grants to data-focused partners, ensuring alignment with Tara’s strategic priorities and program needs.Support good collaboration between data oriented civil society organisations and Tara program teams to ensure that analysis is actionable and responsive to real-world advocacy and policy needs.Miscellaneous:Promote a culture of excellence, service orientation, high performance, and continuous improvementDemonstrate Tara Values in every actionActively participate in Program Team activitiesAbide by Tara’s policies, procedures and practicesAs and when required, other responsibilities and projects will be allocated by line management in consultation with the post holder.Person SpecificationExcited by the prospect of working for a dynamic and fast-growing Asia-based organisation with a mission to build a more prosperous, thriving and sustainable future for Asia, the ideal candidate will have:Essential• Good knowledge of the clean energy transition, particularly in Asia, including familiarity with power sector transformation, renewable energy deployment, fossil fuel phase-down, energy policy, and/or industrial decarbonisation.• Six to ten years of relevant professional experience in data analysis, research, monitoring and evaluation, or a related field, gained in civil society, philanthropy, government, academia, consulting, business or international organisations.• Demonstrated ability to interpret energy and climate data in context, including the ability to identify trends, assess data quality, recognise gaps and draw strategic implications.• Strong quantitative and analytical skills, attention to detail, and experience working with large, complex and imperfect datasets.• Excellent data handling and presentation skills using Microsoft Office 365 applications, especially Excel, PowerPoint and Word. Good skills in analytical tools or languages, such as R, Python, SQL, or statistical software.• Highly developed skills in creating dashboards, reporting tools and data visualisations using platforms such as Tableau, PowerBI, Grantelligence or other similar tools.• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, including the ability to explain complex energy, climate and data analysis concepts to non-specialist audiences.• Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work effectively in a dynamic, cross-cultural, remote team across the Asia Pacific region.• Ability to manage multiple workstreams independently, exercise sound judgement, and deliver high-quality outputs with limited supervision.Desirable• Familiarity with key open-access (eg GEM, Ember) and/or proprietary (eg BNEF) energy related datasets.• Experience working with civil society organisations, philanthropies, think tanks, research institutes or advocacy coalitions.• Familiarity with monitoring, evaluation and learning approaches, especially in complex systems-change, policy, advocacy or philanthropic contexts.• Experience with grant-making, grants management or commissioning external research and analysis.• Knowledge of one or more Asian energy markets, policy environments or political economies.Personal Attributes• Deep intellectual curiosity about energy systems, climate mitigation and the clean energy transition.• Demonstrated motivation to understand not only what the data says, but what it means for strategy, policy, advocacy and real-world change.• A strong commitment to climate action and to supporting a rapid, equitable transition away from fossil fuels.• Ability to combine analytical rigor with practical judgement.• Confidence engaging with both technical experts and non-technical stakeholders.• Strong organisational and self-management skills, particularly in a remote and internationally distributed team.• Flexibility over working hours to accommodate international time zones• Willingness to travel both regionally and internationally on occasion.• High ethical standards and experience handling confidential information.Education• Malaysia, Indonesia, Bangladesh, or Pakistan and must have long-term residency status with legal permit to reside and work in the country.Stakeholder ManagementKey external stakeholders• Tara PartnerKey internal stakeholders• Regional Program team• Program Directors•Senior Program officers•Grants OfficersWhat Tara Foundation can offerThe unique opportunity to work as part of a highly challenge yourselfCompetitive compensation and benefitsA collaborative, fun and thriving team, driven by our mission and live our valuesTo Apply:Please submit an A4 covering letter outlining your skills and achievements against the role responsibilities, why you would like to join us and your salary expectations, along with your up-to-date CV at Recruitment@taraclimate.org.Please use the job title: Senior Data & Impact Officer as the subject in your email.Applications without a cover letter will not be assessed.Tara is committed to building leadership capacity within Asia. This position can be based in Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Philippines, Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia, Bangladesh, or Pakistan, all applicants must have the right to live and work in that country.We appreciate everyone's application but only have the resources to respond to shortlisted applicants. If you have not heard fromus within 3 weeks of the closing date, please assume you are unsuccessful forthis role. If this is you, we appreciate your support and consideration andhope you will continue to consider roles with Tara in the future! Visit us at: https://taraclimate.org/.

job_text:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Founded in 1978 as Singapore's first family service centre, Allkin Singapore is a community-centric social service agency that believes everyone has the potential to create positive change, regardless of age, race, or background.
Through our service offerings, strategic partnerships, and community initiatives, we unlock opportunities for vulnerable individuals and families to overcome life's challenges, build a brighter future, and inspire others to do the same.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Allkin Singapore is a community-driven social service agency dedicated to empowering individuals and families to build a brighter future. We are building our core technical team to drive innovation and digital transformation within the agency and across the social service sector. The team's work will impact tens of thousands of lives in the years to come.
In this role, you will work closely with senior leadership, internal stakeholders, and external partners to identify needs, assess technologies, and design scalable digital solutions that enhance service delivery and organisational effectiveness. You will have the opportunity to collaborate and work with advisors from established entities such as the Open Government Products and Google etc.
This is a hands-on role suited for an experienced full-stack engineer with strong systems integration and data engineering expertise. You will focus on evaluating, adapting, and operationalising existing technologies, rather than building from scratch, while implementing customised systems that strengthen organisational capabilities in a lean and evolving environment.
We require a highly resourceful, adaptive, and self-directed individual who is comfortable operating independently in a lean environment and building foundational capabilities from the ground up. The ideal candidate is motivated not only by technology, but by the opportunity to strengthen the ecosystem of care through thoughtful and sustainable digital transformation.
Key Responsibilities

Digital Solutioning & Systems Integration

Assess organisational workflows, operational pain points, and service delivery needs
Identify, evaluate, and recommend suitable digital technologies, platforms, and tools
Configure, customise, integrate, and operationalise selected technologies to meet agency requirements
Lead end-to-end implementation of digital solutions, including user onboarding and adoption support
Translate non-technical operational requirements into practical technology solutions




Full-Stack Engineering & Technical Implementation

Develop and maintain internal applications, portals, dashboards, automation workflows, and APIs where required
Integrate systems across platforms using APIs, middleware, workflow tools, and data connectors
Support frontend and backend development activities as needed
Ensure solutions are maintainable, secure, scalable, and fit-for-purpose within a resource-constrained environment
Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues independently




Database Engineering

Establish foundational data architecture and data pipelines to support operational reporting and decision-making
Design and maintain relational databases and data storage structures
Consolidate and integrate data from multiple operational systems
Support data quality, governance, and accessibility initiatives
Implement basic reporting and analytics workflows
Continuously strengthen the agency's internal data capabilities and infrastructure



QUALIFICATIONS

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Software Engineering, or related discipline
Minimum 7-8 years of hands-on experience in software engineering or technology implementation roles
Strong full-stack development capabilities across modern frontend and backend technologies, including API development and SaaS/platform integrations
Experience with databases and data management, including data modelling, ETL pipelines, and data integration/transformation processes
We are particularly looking for someone who is:

A self-starter who can operate independently with minimal supervision 
Comfortable navigating ambiguity and building foundational systems in a low-tech environment
Resourceful, pragmatic, and solutions-oriented 
Able to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution
Passionate about leveraging technology to strengthen social impact and service delivery
Curious, adaptable, and committed to continuous learning


Experience in the social service, healthcare, public sector, or non-profit environment would be advantageous, though not mandatory.

OTHER INFORMATION
Liberte HR Services Pte Ltd is the designated recruitment partner for Allkin Singapore. As part of the hiring process, you may receive communication from them regarding this opportunity.

    Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - Allkin Singapore Ltd

job_text: About UsFounded in 2012, ByteDance's mission is to inspire creativity and enrich life. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​Job highlightsCareer growth opportunity, Paid leave, Industry experts, Meals provided, Competitive compensationResponsibilitiesAbout the team（IPD) Strategy and ResearchThe IPD Strategy and Research Department mainly studies external policy changes related to Data center infrastructure and the supply chain, responds to the impacts brought by important macro environment and industrial policy changes, anticipates important trends, and provides corresponding strategies,  builds a globally competitive Data center infrastructure with good economic performance and high resilience .Policy research takes geopolitics as the main clue, with industrial policies and supply chain policies of the Data center infrastructure industry under the global geopolitical backdrop as the focal points.The strategy formulation takes energy as the main line, builds a strategic framework for the energy system, conducts systematic research on the electricity price policies, grid characteristics, green power construction and trading mechanisms in the core regions of global Data center infrastructure, and constructs a combined cost reduction model.Combining research on energy and geopolitics, conduct research on the site selection strategy for Data center infrastructure in key regions.Job Responsibilities1. Conduct systematic research on Southeast Asia’s power markets and formulate electricity strategies suitable for the siting and operation of large-scale data centers.2. Lead research on data center power trading, electricity cost reduction, and compute–power co-optimization, and implement electricity cost-optimization strategies at existing data center sites.3. Develop medium- to long-term green power strategies for data centers in Southeast Asia and plan phased implementation.QualificationsMinimum Qualifications:1. Bachelor’s degree or above with 5+ years of relevant work experience; experience in large energy enterprises, internet companies’ energy management departments, or the energy consulting industry is preferred.2. Familiarity with Southeast Asia’s power sector and market trading systems, as well as policies and development trends related to the Southeast Asia data center market.3. Deep understanding of mainstream vendors’ power strategies and solutions in Southeast Asia, with the ability to support the execution of data center site-selection strategies. job_text: Business FunctionGroup Technology empowers the bank with an efficient, nimble and resilient infrastructure and system through a strategic focus on productivity, quality & control, technology, coupled with strengthening people capability and banking on innovation. In Group Technology, we manage the majority of the Bank's operational processes and inspire to delight our business partners and customers through our multiple banking delivery channels.We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Model Owner to join our Technology Data Chapter. This role is crucial in ensuring the responsible development, deployment, and ongoing monitoring of AI/ML models/GenAI solutions. The Model Owner will play a pivotal role in upholding DBS's Responsible Data Use (RDU) framework, particularly the PURE principles (Purposeful, Unsurprising, Respectful, Explainable) and AI Governance standards.Job ResponsibilitiesThe Model Owner will be accountable and responsible for the entire lifecycle of assigned models, working closely with various stakeholders to ensure their fitness for purpose and adherence to governance requirements. Key responsibilities include:Model Lifecycle Management: Own the development, deployment, and monitoring of AI/ML models/GenAI solution throughout their life cycle.Requirements Translation: Ensure that business requirements from the Use Case Owner are clearly communicated to the model development team and that models are developed according to these requirements.Model Evaluation & Testing: Conduct comprehensive model testing and review performance and fairness results (where applicable) to ensure models are fit for purpose and ready for deployment.Communication & Training: Ensure necessary communication and user training on model outputs, expected usage, assumptions, and limitations are provided to the use case team.Data Quality Assurance: Ensure that critical data attributes for the model are identified and periodically quality-checked to meet defined data quality thresholds.Governance & Compliance: Ensure all owned models adhere to the AI governance framework, unit and location-specific requirements, and other internal and external policies and regulations.Third-Party Model Oversight (Proxy Model Owner): If a third-party AI model (e.g., ecosystem partner model or subscription-based model) is used where DBS does not own or have access to the model, a Proxy Model Owner will be identified. This role focuses on the evaluation and ongoing monitoring of the model to ensure its fitness for purpose, conducting appropriate due diligence, and reviewing vendor documentation and processes. Job RequirementsProven experience in AI/ML model/GenAI solution/Agentic AI development, deployment, and monitoring within a data-intensive environment.Strong understanding of AI/ML models/GenAI/Agentic AI solution, data quality principles, and relevant governance frameworks.Familiarity with responsible data use principles and AI governance standards, such as MAS FEAT principles.Ability to conduct thorough model testing, performance review, and fairness assessments.Excellent communication skills to articulate concepts to diverse audiences, provide user training, and collaborate effectively with stakeholders.Strong problem-solving abilities to identify and mitigate risks associated with model usage.A deep understanding and commitment to ethical data practices and responsible AI development.Apply Now​We offera competitive salary and benefits package and the professional advantages of adynamic environment that supports your development and recognises yourachievements.
